14.reason: [Error: The scanf reads into a[n].x1, then increments n, then reads into a[n].x2 (which is a different element), etc. This corrupts the array. Also, the loop condition uses EOF but the problem specifies reading a fixed number of segments from the first line.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The scanf reads into a[n].x1, a[n].x2, a[n].y1, a[n].y2 but increments n each time, so it reads into different array elements instead of the same one. This causes incorrect data storage and potential out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Input reading is incorrect. The problem specifies first line is number of segments, not EOF. Additionally, the program fails to read the initial number of segments specified in the input format, leading to complete data misalignment.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The input reading logic incorrectly increments 'n' four times per segment, causing wrong indexing and overwriting data. It should read all four values for a[n] then increment n once.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The input reading logic incorrectly increments 'n' four times per segment, causing wrong indexing and skipping of array elements. It should read all four values for a[n] before incrementing n once.]
